questions.title: Find Maximum in Array
questions.description: Write a function to find the maximum element in an array of integers.
questions.inputFormat: First line: integer N. Second line: N space-separated integers.
questions.explanation: Initialize max with first element, iterate through array, update max if current element is greater.
questions.starterCode:
def find_max(arr):
    # Your code here
    pass

n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
print(find_max(arr))
hints: Compare each element with current max
Update max if current element is larger

questions.title: Reverse Array
questions.description: Write a function to reverse an array in-place.
questions.inputFormat: First line: integer N. Second line: N space-separated integers.
questions.outputFormat: Print the reversed array elements separated by space.
questions.explanation: Swap elements from start and end moving towards center. Swap arr[i] with arr[n-1-i].
questions.starterCode:
def reverse_array(arr):
    # Your code here
    pass

n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
reverse_array(arr)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
questions.solution:
def reverse_array(arr):
    left, right = 0, len(arr) - 1
    while left < right:
        arr[left], arr[right] = arr[right], arr[left]
        left += 1
        right -= 1

n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
reverse_array(arr)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
hints: Use two pointers: start and end

questions.title: Check Anagram
questions.description: Write a function to check if two strings are anagrams of each other. Anagrams have same characters with same frequency.
questions.inputFormat: Two lines, each containing a string.
questions.constraints: String length: 1 to 1000, only lowercase letters.
questions.explanation: Count frequency of each character in both strings. Compare the frequency counts.
questions.starterCode:
def is_anagram(s1, s2):
    # Your code here
    pass

s1 = input().strip()
s2 = input().strip()
print(str(is_anagram(s1, s2)).lower())
hints: Use array to count character frequencies
Increment for first string, decrement for second

questions.title: Rotate Array
questions.description: Given an array, rotate it to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative. Do it in-place with O(1) extra space.
questions.inputFormat: First line: N and k separated by space. Second line: N space-separated integers.
questions.outputFormat: Print the rotated array elements separated by space.
questions.explanation: Use reversal method: reverse entire array, reverse first k elements, reverse remaining elements. Handle k > n by k = k % n.
questions.starterCode:
def rotate(arr, k):
    # Your code here
    pass

n, k = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
rotate(arr, k)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
questions.solution:
def reverse(arr, start, end):
    while start < end:
        arr[start], arr[end] = arr[end], arr[start]
        start += 1
        end -= 1

def rotate(arr, k):
    n = len(arr)
    if n == 0:
        return
    k = k % n
    reverse(arr, 0, n - 1)
    reverse(arr, 0, k - 1)
    reverse(arr, k, n - 1)

n, k = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
rotate(arr, k)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
